response.justification: Bogdan Szabo is an experienced full-stack and web engineer with a broad background, but he is fundamentally misaligned with this role's core requirements. The position demands native-level Japanese proficiency—an absolute prerequisite—which is entirely absent from his profile. He has no demonstrable experience in embedded firmware, LAN-switch or embedded networking products, Layer-2/Layer-3 security features, or any of the listed security protocols (MACsec, 802.1X, Captive Portal). His C/C++ exposure is minimal and incidental (one short NI role and a bachelor thesis), whereas the role requires deep C proficiency for UNIX/embedded development. His career is predominantly oriented toward TypeScript, Node.js, web platforms, and developer tooling—a fundamentally different domain.
strengths: 15+ years of software development experience across diverse stacks, demonstrating adaptability and seniority
Some C and C++ exposure (National Instruments, Sound Position Server thesis using Linux and C++)
Familiarity with Linux and some embedded/low-level context (Sociomantic low-latency async I/O, NI LabWindows)
concerns: No Japanese language proficiency whatsoever—this is a hard, non-negotiable requirement for this role
Zero experience in embedded networking, LAN-switch firmware, or any of the security protocols specified (MACsec, 802.1X, NETCONF, SNMP, Captive Portal)
C programming is not a primary skill; career is built on TypeScript/Node.js/web with only peripheral and outdated C/C++ use

key_factors.factor: No embedded networking or switch security experience
